---
title: "cleaning"
format: html
editor: source
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

```


loop through and combine all datasets into one
```{r}
file_list <- list.files(path = c("data/woodside"), pattern = "\\.txt$", full.names = TRUE)
data_list <- list()

library(foreach)
library(dplyr)

final_data <- foreach(current_file = file_list, .combine = 'bind_rows') %do% {
  
  # The fix: set row.names = NULL and header = TRUE
  df <- read.csv(current_file, 
                 header = TRUE,
                 row.names = NULL, 
                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE, 
                 check.names = FALSE) # check.names=FALSE prevents mangling XC_Jump_Pen
  
  # IMPORTANT: When row.names = NULL, read.csv sometimes shifts 
  # columns to the right. We fix the column names here:
  if(colnames(df)[1] == "row.names") {
    colnames(df) <- colnames(df)[2:length(colnames(df))]
    df <- df[, 1:(ncol(df)-1)]
  }
  
  # Add the filename column using the tool that handles extensions
  df$event_info <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(current_file))
  
  df
}



for (i in seq_along(file_list)) {
 
   current_file <- file_list[1]
  
  df <- read.csv(current_file, 
               stringsAsFactors = FALSE, 
               check.names = TRUE)
  
  # Add the filename column
  df <- df %>% 
    mutate(event_info = basename(current_file))
  
  df$event_info <- basename(current_file)
  
  # Store it in our list
  data_list[[i]] <- df
  
  # Optional: Print progress
  message(paste("Processed file", i, "of", length(file_list)))
}

# 3. Combine and Save
# bind_rows is significantly faster than do.call(rbind, ...)
final_data <- bind_rows(data_list)

write_csv(final_data, "data/woodside_combined_data.csv")
```


keep only training, prelim, and modified
